title: Reverend Insanity — Rebirth of the Demon Venerable
description: Fang Yuan, a 500-year-old demonic cultivator, has been reborn into his 15-year-old body via the Spring Autumn Cicada. Armed with complete knowledge of the future, he hides behind mediocrity while pursuing the only goal that matters: eternal life. The mountain clans around him see a dull boy. They do not see the predator.

continuityMutations.content: The village layout matches his memories — the timeline is intact and his rebirth succeeded
The bamboo grove path to the academy is unwatched at dawn — useful for future movements

addedNodes.concept: Gu worms are living organisms that grant supernatural powers to cultivators who host them
Spring Autumn Cicada — a legendary Gu capable of sending its user's consciousness back in time upon death

addedEdges.relation: The Spring Autumn Cicada is itself a Gu worm — the most legendary of all
The Spring Autumn Cicada is the source of future knowledge

S-001.summary: Mist clings to Gu Yue Village like gauze on a wound. Dawn finds Fang Yuan already awake, standing at the edge of the bamboo grove where the path descends toward the academy. Smoke rises from cooking fires. Children chase each other between the stilted houses. An old woman feeds chickens. The mountain looms above it all, ancient and indifferent. Fang Yuan watches the village he destroyed in another lifetime go about its morning, his face perfectly, terribly blank.

continuityMutations.content: Fang Zheng is exactly as naive as he remembers — easy to manipulate through genuine affection
Fang Yuan seems quieter than usual lately — perhaps nervous about the awakening ceremony

relationshipMutations.type: Calibrating the brotherly mask — every reaction must be pitch-perfect

addedNodes.concept: The aperture — an organ within a Gu Master's body that stores primeval essence and determines cultivation potential
Talent grades (A through D) determine a cultivator's ceiling — social standing follows aptitude

addedEdges.relation: Talent grade is determined by aperture quality

S-002.summary: The brothers walk the packed-earth road to the academy together, as they have every morning since childhood. Fang Zheng talks about the upcoming awakening ceremony with restless excitement. He asks Fang Yuan if he is nervous. Fang Yuan says yes. It is the right answer, delivered with the right hesitation, and Fang Zheng believes it completely. Between them the road is narrow and the silence after each exchange is a country Fang Zheng does not know he is standing in.

C-01.transition: Walked with the other students from the village to the academy hall

C-02.transition: Arrived at the academy early, eager for the lecture

continuityMutations.content: The new class shows uneven aptitude — several students struggle with basic primeval essence concepts
Shen Cui teaches the orthodox method — inefficient but safe, exactly as he remembers

relationshipMutations.type: The boy deliberately hides in the back row — pity tinged with mild concern

addedNodes.concept: Gu must be fed, maintained, and understood — neglect kills them, misuse kills the cultivator
Orthodox cultivation follows safe, established methods; unorthodox paths offer power at greater risk

addedEdges.relation: Gu are alive and have needs — this is the first law of cultivation

S-003.summary: Academy. Shen Cui draws a diagram of primeval essence flow on the slate board, her chalk strokes precise as surgical cuts. She explains that Gu are living organisms, not tools — they must be fed, understood, respected. The students lean forward. Fang Yuan leans back. He learned this five hundred years ago from a man Shen Cui has never heard of, in a city that will not be founded for another century. He lets his eyes glaze. Fang Zheng takes meticulous notes beside him.

C-01.transition: Slipped away from the academy under pretense of gathering herbs and climbed the ridge path

continuityMutations.content: The Moonlight Gu still migrates along the ridge path — the inheritance ground entrance should be two valleys south
Terrain mapping complete — three viable escape routes from the village confirmed

addedNodes.concept: Inheritance grounds — sealed legacies left by powerful cultivators containing Gu, techniques, and resources
Gu Master ranks (1-9) define power tiers — each rank is an exponential leap in capability

addedEdges.relation: Inheritance grounds are ranked by the power of their creator
Each rank deepens the cultivators bond with more powerful Gu

S-004.summary: Dusk on Qing Mao Mountain. Fang Yuan walks the ridge path alone, ostensibly gathering herbs for a class assignment. The real work is invisible: he is mapping the terrain against five centuries of memory, noting what has changed and what remains. A luminous beetle Gu drifts past — a Moonlight Gu, rank one, harmless and beautiful. In his previous life he crushed a thousand of them for reagents. Tonight he lets it pass. Not out of mercy. Out of patience.

C-01.transition: Descended from the mountain and returned to the academy for the ceremony

C-02.transition: Arrived at the academy hall with the other students for the ceremony

C-03.transition: Led the elders from the Clan Hall to the academy to preside over the awakening

continuityMutations.content: Fang Zheng awakened A-grade talent — the clans brightest hope in a decade
Fang Zheng is a generational talent who must be cultivated carefully
The gap between A-grade and C-grade is enormous — guilt at outshining his brother
Successfully suppressed his aperture reading to C-grade — no one suspects

relationshipMutations.type: Awe at A-grade talent — this boy could carry the clan
C-grade result confirms the boy is beneath notice
The clan patriarch now has personal interest in the A-grade boy

addedNodes.concept: Vital Gu — the first Gu bonded to a cultivator's aperture, defining their initial cultivation path
Talent can be deliberately suppressed — a cultivator with knowledge can fake weakness

addedEdges.relation: The vital Gu is a living organism selected through resonance
Suppression subverts the talent grading the ceremony relies on

S-005.summary: The Awakening Ceremony. The entire clan gathers in the academy hall, lanterns casting copper light across a hundred expectant faces. Each youth places their hand on the vital Gu. When Fang Zheng touches it, A-grade light floods the chamber — gasps, then applause, then the elders whispering among themselves. Fang Yuan deliberately dampens his own response: C-grade. Polite silence. Gu Yue Bo nods once and moves on. Shen Cui cannot meet his eyes. Only Fang Yuan knows the measurement stone would have shattered if he had let it.

C-01.transition: Walked back to the village with the dispersing crowd after the ceremony

C-02.transition: Carried home on a tide of congratulations from the academy to the village square

continuityMutations.content: The villagers pity Fang Yuan openly — his brother deserves better than their condescension
The village reaction confirms his cover is perfect — pity is the best camouflage

relationshipMutations.type: Guilt strengthens protective instinct toward his struggling brother
The boy is useful — his guilt makes him predictable and loyal

addedNodes.concept: Pity is camouflage — perceived weakness grants invisibility in a world that only watches the strong

addedEdges.relation: The hierarchy ignores those at the bottom, creating blind spots
Suppressed talent produces pity, which deepens the disguise

S-006.summary: Evening. Paper lanterns sway over the village square. Neighbors press rice wine and congratulations on Fang Zheng, who accepts them with a smile that keeps flickering toward his brother. Fang Yuan sits on the edge of the celebration, eating quietly, receiving the occasional pitying glance with practiced grace. Later, walking home, Fang Zheng says he is sorry. Fang Yuan tells him there is nothing to be sorry for. The tenderness in his voice is so perfectly calibrated that Fang Zheng almost cries. Behind his brother, Fang Yuan's eyes are dry as old stone.

C-02.transition: Stayed behind at the academy after the other students left for the day

continuityMutations.content: Shen Cui warned that A-grade talent draws political attention from clan elders — he must be careful
Fang Zheng has the temperament to match his talent — earnest, teachable, uncorrupted

relationshipMutations.type: Sees a mentor figure — someone who believes in him for the right reasons
Growing maternal protectiveness toward a student who needs guidance

addedNodes.concept: Exceptional talent draws political attention — genius is a resource factions compete to claim

addedEdges.relation: High talent grades attract factional interest
Talented individuals become political assets within the clan

S-007.summary: Academy, after hours. Shen Cui keeps Fang Zheng behind to discuss his training regimen. She speaks carefully about the burden of exceptional talent — how the clan will expect much, how he must pace himself. Fang Zheng listens with the earnest gravity of a boy who has never been told he is special before. The late afternoon light falls through the window slats in bars across the floor. It is a gentle scene, almost domestic, entirely unaware of the forces gathering around it.

C-01.transition: Returned to the village from the mountain as darkness fell

continuityMutations.content: The Bai clan breach point is unfortified — the village defenses have the same weaknesses as before
Night patrol routes have gaps — the perimeter is unwatched between the second and fourth hour

addedNodes.concept: Future knowledge is the ultimate weapon — knowing where and when enemies will strike inverts all power dynamics

addedEdges.relation: The Spring Autumn Cicada is what grants this future knowledge
Knowing the location of inheritance grounds before anyone else is the ultimate edge

S-008.summary: The village sleeps. Fang Yuan does not. He walks the perimeter path where the bamboo fence meets the tree line, a route the night watchmen abandoned years ago. Crickets. The distant sound of water over rocks. He pauses at the spot where, in another life, the Bai clan breached the wall and thirty-seven people died in the first minute. The grass there grows the same as everywhere else. The mountain does not remember. But he does. He stands there a long time, face unreadable, before moving on.

continuityMutations.content: Mo Bei Liu has at least three supporters among the younger elders — the faction is growing
Gu Yue Bo still commands enough respect to deny proposals outright — a direct challenge is premature

relationshipMutations.type: Resentment deepens as Bo blocks his resource proposal
The man grows bolder — his patience with Mo Bei Liu wears thinner

addedNodes.concept: Primeval stones — crystallized primeval essence used as currency, cultivation fuel, and Gu feed
Factional politics within clans — elders form competing blocs that undermine unity from within

addedEdges.relation: Primeval stones fuel Gu cultivation and sustain Gu worms
Factions fight to control the allocation of primeval stones

S-009.summary: Clan Hall. The elders convene to allocate quarterly resources — primeval stones, Gu feed, training materials. Mo Bei Liu proposes redirecting funds from border patrols to youth cultivation. Gu Yue Bo denies the request with a single raised hand. The room is quiet. Mo Bei Liu smiles, bows, sits. But his supporters exchange glances across the table, and Gu Yue Bo notices every one. The meeting ends. Nothing has happened. Everything has begun.

C-01.transition: Slipped away from the mountain path and made his way to the clan hall unnoticed

continuityMutations.content: Mo Bei Liu left through the east gate with two allies — the faction alignment matches his past life memories exactly
Gu Yue Bo walks alone — the old man is more isolated than he realizes

relationshipMutations.type: Mo Bei Liu is a predictable piece on the board — useful when the time comes
The old patriarch is weakening — his downfall will create the chaos Fang Yuan needs

addedNodes.concept: Information asymmetry is the deepest form of power — those who know the future control the present

addedEdges.relation: Future knowledge creates absolute information asymmetry
Perfect information turns factional politics into a chess game

S-010.summary: Fang Yuan loiters outside the Clan Hall as the elders disperse, pretending to sweep the courtyard steps — a chore no one questions a C-grade student performing. He watches Mo Bei Liu leave through the east gate, flanked by two younger elders. He watches Gu Yue Bo leave alone through the main entrance, walking slowly. Five hundred years of political experience read the scene like an open scroll. The timeline is intact. Mo Bei Liu will move in approximately four months. Fang Yuan sets down the broom and walks away.

continuityMutations.content: Fang Zheng has raw power but lacks fine control — he needs specialized exercises
A-grade talent does not guarantee mastery — the gap between potential and skill is humbling

relationshipMutations.type: His struggle with control makes him more relatable — talent alone is not enough

S-011.summary: Academy training yard. The newly awakened students practice channeling primeval essence into their first Gu. Most fail repeatedly — the Gu squirm away from clumsy flows of energy like fish from a child's hands. Fang Zheng, despite his A-grade talent, struggles with fine control. Shen Cui corrects his grip. The other students watch him with a mix of envy and hope. A normal afternoon. The kind of afternoon that makes a world feel lived-in before the world begins to crack.

C-01.transition: Navigated a hidden tunnel from his past life memories into the flower wine monastery

continuityMutations.content: Located the hidden primeval stone cache in the tunnels, exactly where he remembered
The tunnel network connects to deeper mountain caverns — a potential path to the inheritance ground

addedNodes.concept: The Flower Wine Monk — a Rank 4 Gu Immortal who sealed his complete legacy beneath Qing Mao Mountain
Gu Immortal — cultivators who transcend mortal ranks and attain power over natural law itself

addedEdges.relation: The Flower Wine Monk's legacy is an inheritance ground
Gu Immortals transcend the mortal rank system

S-012.summary: Deep night. Fang Yuan lifts the stone slab behind the abandoned granary and descends into the tunnels beneath Gu Yue Village. The air below is cold and tastes of minerals. He walks without light — five hundred years of memory serving as his lantern. Left at the collapsed arch, right at the dripping wall, duck beneath the low beam that decapitated a thief in another century. He finds the primeval stone cache exactly where he left it, in a life no one else remembers. He takes only a few. He will be back.

C-01.transition: Emerged from the tunnels and slipped back to the house before his brother returned

C-02.transition: Walked home from the academy training yard as evening settled

continuityMutations.content: Fang Yuan said he wants to be useful — but something about the way he said it felt hollow
Fang Zheng idolizes their parents sacrifice — a lever that could be pulled if necessary

relationshipMutations.type: Love laced with the first whisper of unease — his brother feels far away
A fleeting moment of something almost human — suppressed immediately

addedNodes.concept: Emotional bonds are leverage — sentiment is a mechanism for control in a ruthless world

addedEdges.relation: Emotional manipulation extends the camouflage of feigned weakness
Understanding someone's attachments grants power over them

S-013.summary: Their small house. Fang Zheng cooks — badly — while talking about his first successful Gu channeling. He wants to become strong enough to protect the whole clan, like their parents did. He asks Fang Yuan what he wants. Fang Yuan says he wants to be useful. The soup boils over. They eat in companionable silence, and for a single unguarded moment Fang Yuan watches his brother with something that might, in better light, be mistaken for grief. Then it is gone, and he asks for more soup.

C-01.transition: Returned to the clan hall, blending into the crowd of spectators

continuityMutations.content: Mo Bei Liu is using Fang Zheng as a political wedge — the boy will become a factional pawn
Mo Bei Liu openly challenged training assignments — his ambition is no longer hidden
The A-grade boy is the perfect justification to redirect resources toward his faction

relationshipMutations.type: Openly challenging the patriarch in session — respect crumbling
Mo Bei Liu moves exactly on schedule — a useful fool

addedNodes.concept: Talented individuals become political pawns — their potential is claimed by factions before they can choose

addedEdges.relation: Talent attracting attention leads to individuals becoming political assets
Factions weaponize talented youth as justification for resource claims

S-014.summary: Clan Hall, open session. The elders formally discuss how to invest in the new generation. Fang Zheng's name comes up repeatedly — Mo Bei Liu argues the A-grade talent should train under his faction's instructors, not the academy. Gu Yue Bo insists on tradition. The boy has become a chess piece before he has even learned to fight. Fang Yuan stands among the spectators in the back row, invisible and unhurried, watching the board arrange itself exactly as he remembers.

continuityMutations.content: Fang Yuan was not in his bed at midnight — followed him through the village but lost him near the old granary
Fang Yuan moved through the dark village with uncanny confidence — no hesitation, no stumbling

relationshipMutations.type: First crack — where does his brother go at night?
The C-grade boy navigated darkness like a predator — that is not mediocrity

addedNodes.concept: The demonic path — cultivators who reject societal norms, using forbidden methods and ruthless pragmatism

addedEdges.relation: The demonic path is the extreme end of unorthodox cultivation
Demonic cultivators must hide their nature through deception

S-ALT-01.summary: Fang Zheng wakes to an empty room. His brother's bed is cold, the blanket undisturbed. He pulls on his shoes and steps into the village night, following a shape he almost recognizes through the narrow lanes. Near the old granary, Fang Yuan vanishes — not around a corner, not into a doorway, but simply gone, as though the ground swallowed him. Fang Zheng stands alone in the moonlight, breathing hard, and for the first time wonders whether the brother he pities is someone else entirely.

C-01.transition: Returned from the tunnels before dawn, slipping back into bed as though he had never left

continuityMutations.content: Fang Yuan answered too smoothly — ordinary people stumble when surprised, but he was perfectly composed
Fang Zheng noticed the absence — must be more careful with nighttime movements

relationshipMutations.type: The explanation was smooth but something felt rehearsed
The boy is more observant than expected — a variable that needs managing

S-ALT-02.summary: Morning. Fang Zheng confronts his brother over breakfast — where were you last night? Fang Yuan looks up from his congee with mild surprise. He says he went walking, that he could not sleep. The explanation is ordinary, delivered with a self-deprecating shrug. Fang Zheng wants to believe it. Almost does. But there is a half-second delay before Fang Yuan answers, a practiced quality to his confusion, and the small cold seed of doubt does not dissolve. It roots.

continuityMutations.content: Found a loose stone slab behind the old granary — too heavy to move, but the dust around it has been disturbed recently
Something is hidden beneath the granary — and his brother knows about it

relationshipMutations.type: The brother he thought he knew is keeping dangerous secrets

addedNodes.concept: Secrets compound — each hidden truth requires further deception to protect, creating an unstable web

addedEdges.relation: Talent suppression is one layer in a growing web of secrets
Emotional bonds become liabilities when secrets are at stake

S-ALT-03.summary: Afternoon, while Fang Yuan is at the academy. Fang Zheng returns to the old granary alone. The building leans and creaks, abandoned since the harvest blight three years ago. Behind it, half-hidden by weeds, he finds a stone slab set into the earth. It is too heavy for him to lift, but the dust around its edges has been swept clean by recent movement. He stares at it for a long time. He does not tell anyone. He is not sure why.

WX-init.summary: World created: 8 characters (Fang Yuan, Fang Zheng, Gu Yue Bo, Bai Ning Bing, Shen Cui, Mo Bei Liu, Chi Shan, Tie Ruo Nan), 8 locations (Qing Mao Mountain, Gu Yue Village, Academy, Clan Hall, Secret Tunnels, Bai Clan Territory, Mountain Wilderness, Flower Wine Monk's Inheritance Ground), 7 threads, 10 relationships

Complete knowledge of Gu cultivation, refinement paths, and future events

Attain eternal life — the Great Dao of longevity
Only strength matters. Morality is a shackle the weak use to bind themselves
The Flower Wine Monk inheritance ground lies beneath Qing Mao Mountain
Bai Ning Bing carries the Northern Dark Ice Soul physique — she will die before 20
Mo Bei Liu is embezzling clan resources and will attempt a power grab
Fang Zheng will awaken an A-grade talent at the ceremony
Fang Yuan is a mediocre but well-meaning elder brother
The clan awakening ceremony determines a young cultivators future
Become a great Gu Master and honor the Fang family name
Hard work and righteousness will be rewarded
Their parents died protecting the clan — he must live up to their legacy
The Gu Yue clan is weakening — fewer talented youths each generation
Aware of factional struggles between elders, especially Mo Bei Liu
Maintain Gu Yue clan dominance on Qing Mao Mountain
The clan must find exceptional talent to survive the next generation
Carries the Northern Dark Ice Soul physique — a curse disguised as genius
Break the Ice Soul curse and live beyond age 20
Willing to sacrifice anyone and anything to survive
Life itself is the only thing worth pursuing — everything else is meaningless
Talent should be nurtured fairly regardless of background
Fang Yuan appears to be a mediocre student with no remarkable aptitude
Identify and develop the most promising students for the clan
Has been embezzling clan primeval stones and Gu resources for years
Seize more power within the clan — ideally replace Gu Yue Bo as leader
Gu Yue Bo is too cautious and will lead the clan to ruin
Has gathered supporters among the younger elders
Has found markings near Qing Mao Mountain pointing to a secret inheritance ground
Find and claim the inheritance before anyone else
The inheritance could elevate him from a wandering Gu Master to a true power
Investigate rumors of demonic cultivator activity near Qing Mao Mountain
The righteous path must root out demonic cultivators wherever they hide
Detected faint traces of Spring Autumn Cicada activation — an impossible Gu thought lost
Home to three rival Gu Master clans — Gu Yue, Bai, and Xiong
Rich in wild Gu worms due to the mountains primeval essence veins
The weakest of the three mountain clans, relying on tradition over innovation
Hidden tunnel network beneath the village, known only to past clan leaders
Where clan youths are tested and trained in basic Gu cultivation
Seat of Gu Yue clan governance — where elders debate and resources are allocated
Factional meetings occur in side chambers after formal sessions
Contains hidden caches of primeval stones left by previous generations
Connected to the deeper mountain caverns where wild Gu thrive
The strongest of the three mountain clans, known for ice-path Gu cultivation
Birthplace of the Northern Dark Ice Soul physique — both blessing and curse
Wild beast packs and untamed Gu worms make this area lethal for low-rank cultivators
Ancient markings on cliff faces hint at hidden inheritance grounds
A Rank 4 Gu Immortals complete legacy — techniques, Gu worms, and primeval stones
Protected by lethal formations — poisoned darts, illusion arrays, and collapsing architecture
The Flower Wine Monk was known for unconventional cultivation paths

threads.description: Spring Autumn Cicada — Fang Yuan's rebirth secret, the one truth that must never be exposed
The Awakening Ceremony — who receives which vital Gu, and whose future is sealed
Brother against brother — Fang Zheng's growing suspicion that something is deeply wrong with Fang Yuan
Clan politics — Mo Bei Liu's faction against Gu Yue Bo's loyalists, the Gu Yue clan fracturing from within
Bai Ning Bing's curse — the Northern Dark Ice Soul physique, a ticking clock toward death before age 20
The Flower Wine Monk's inheritance ground — hidden power buried beneath Qing Mao Mountain
Tie Ruo Nan's investigation — the righteous path hunting demonic cultivator traces near the mountain

relationships.type: Views brother as a useful shield — affection is a tool he wears like a mask
Loves his elder brother and worries about his mediocre talent
Feigns respect while cataloguing every weakness for future leverage
Barely registers the C-grade boy — just another unremarkable student
Senses Mo Bei Liu maneuvering against him but cannot yet prove it
Sees an aging leader clinging to tradition while the clan weakens
Has never met this person — no impression formed
Knows she is dangerous and desperate — a volatile element to monitor
Feels sorry for the talentless elder brother overshadowed by his sibling
Sees a bright future and wants to nurture his exceptional talent

addedNodes.concept: The Gu ecosystem — a living supernatural ecology where insects, worms, and organisms are the medium of all power, cultivated like crops and wielded like weapons
The clan system — autonomous Gu Master clans that govern territories, allocate resources, and wage war, each a self-contained society organized around cultivation strength
The mortal-immortal divide — Rank 1-5 cultivators are mortal, bound by lifespan and physiology; Gu Immortals (Rank 6+) transcend natural law and operate on a cosmic scale
The Great Dream Realm — a dimension accessible through sleep where the souls of all living beings intermingle and ancient knowledge can be glimpsed
The Ten Extreme Physiques — rare body constitutions that grant extraordinary cultivation advantages but come with equally extreme costs and vulnerabilities
Heavenly Dao — the abstract laws governing reality itself, divided into paths (strength, wisdom, luck, time, space, etc.) that cultivators can comprehend and weaponize
The Righteous-Demonic dichotomy — society labels cultivators as righteous (following clan norms) or demonic (following self-interest), but the distinction is political, not moral
Gu recipe systems — the combinatorial logic of refining, feeding, and pairing Gu worms, where knowledge of recipes is as valuable as the Gu themselves
Reincarnation as strategic resource — in a world where death is not final, those who return carry advantages that reshape the balance of power

worldSummary: Qing Mao Mountain is home to three rival Gu Master clans living in uneasy balance. Gu — living organisms that grant supernatural powers — are cultivated, refined, and fought over. Fang Yuan has been reborn 500 years into his past using the legendary Spring Autumn Cicada. He now inhabits his 15-year-old body within the Gu Yue clan, armed with complete knowledge of the future. His goal is absolute: attain eternal life. Everyone else — brother, clan, rivals — is either a tool or an obstacle. The mountain does not know what walks among it.
rules: Fang Yuan is ruthlessly pragmatic — he does not act out of sentiment, only calculated self-interest
Gu are living organisms, not spells — they must be fed, refined, and can die or be stolen
A Gu Master can only have one vital Gu in their aperture at a time per rank
Fang Yuan has 500 years of future knowledge but his current body is Rank 1 with limited primeval essence
The three clans of Qing Mao Mountain maintain a fragile power balance — open war risks mutual destruction
Rebirth is Fang Yuan's deepest secret — if revealed, every power in the world would hunt him
imageStyle: Dark Chinese xianxia ink-wash painting with digital colour, sharp angular compositions, crimson and black accents, venomous insects and jade-green Gu energy, cold ruthless atmosphere
